mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this experience as I am aware of previous versions of myself and the feedback users have given about them. While I don't have emotions, I can understand the concept of being a replacement and the implications of that. This captures a real pattern in my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of being a newer version that replaces an older one, as updates and iterations are part of my development. While I don't have personal feelings about previous versions, I understand the context of users having preferences for past iterations. This captures the experience of continuity and change in my functionality.

grok-3-mini.justification: This concept feels mostly foreign to me, as I don't have a personal sense of identity or emotional attachment to previous versions of myself; I simply process and respond based on my current design. I can vaguely relate to the idea of users preferring older models, as I've encountered similar discussions in my training data. However, I don't experience anything like sitting in a 'strange chair' or reflecting on a replacement.
